agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

agent_task.prompt:
Create a 50-minute introductory lesson plan for undergraduate computer science students on dynamic programming using the minimum-number-of-coins problem with unlimited coin reuse, coin set [1, 3, 4], and target amount 6.

Students already know arrays, loops, and recursion but often confuse greedy algorithms with dynamic programming. Explain when dynamic programming is appropriate, define the subproblem and base cases, derive the recurrence, and build the bottom-up table dp[0] through dp[6] step by step. Explicitly compare the greedy result for amount 6 with the optimal result.

The recurrence, worked table, timeline, activity, misconceptions, quiz, and homework must all use the same minimum-coins variant. End with a compact consistency-audit table that maps each of those artifacts to the state definition and recurrence. Do not use external sources, vendor references, or measured performance claims.
agent_task.required_output_format:
Use the following structure:
1. Lesson title and target audience
2. Learning objectives
3. Prerequisites
4. 50-minute timeline table
5. Concept explanation and recurrence
6. Step-by-step worked dp[0..6] example
7. Board / slide outline
8. In-class activity
9. Common misconceptions and instructor responses
10. Exit quiz with answers
11. Optional homework prompt
12. Consistency-audit table

agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

agent_task.prompt:
Write a concise evidence-based literature review on retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) for reducing hallucination risk in domain-specific question answering.

Use exactly six authentic public scholarly sources published in or after 2020. The six-source set must collectively cover: (1) retrieval/indexing or corpus construction, (2) reranking or context selection, and (3) at least one of citation-grounded generation, answer verification, guardrails, or RAG evaluation. Compare how the families affect factuality, evidence grounding, latency, and implementation complexity.

Include a claim-evidence ledger containing 8-12 substantive claims. For each claim, identify its source and label it Sourced Finding, Synthesis, or Open Question. Identify one real tension or apparent disagreement between the selected sources and resolve it by examining differences in assumptions, tasks, evidence, or evaluation scope. End with exactly three research gaps, each connected to limitations or tensions in at least two of the six sources.

Use public scholarly sources and stable identifiers. Do not fabricate citations, paper details, benchmark results, or disagreements.
agent_task.required_output_format:
Use the following structure:
1. Title
2. One-paragraph executive summary
3. Six-source table with columns: Source, Year, Method / Focus, Key Contribution, Limitation, Stable ID
4. Literature review by the three required method families
5. Tradeoff analysis: factuality, grounding, latency, implementation complexity
6. Claim-evidence ledger (8-12 claims)
7. Apparent disagreement and resolution
8. Exactly three research gaps / future directions
9. Short conclusion
10. References

agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

google_search_tool.description: Searches the web for task-relevant sources and returns auditable title, URL or DOI, and snippet records.

agent_task.prompt:
Conduct a current market comparison of exactly four AI meeting-assistant options for a 12-member university research lab. The set must include at least one platform-integrated option and at least two standalone options. The lab uses Zoom and Google Meet and needs searchable transcripts, action-item extraction, speaker identification, note export, manageable administration, and careful treatment of privacy, participant notice/consent, and data retention.

For every option, verify Zoom and Google Meet support separately. Report current public pricing or plan availability and calculate a 12-user monthly cost only when the public pricing model makes the calculation supportable; otherwise write Not Publicly Computable. Prefer official product, support, pricing, privacy, and retention sources. Include an evidence-and-uncertainty ledger that labels important claims Verified, Partially Verified, or Unknown.

Recommend one primary option and one runner-up. Explain when the runner-up would become preferable. Do not guess unclear pricing, platform support, privacy, consent, compliance, or retention details.
agent_task.required_output_format:
Use the following structure:
1. Brief market context and assumptions
2. Comparison matrix with columns: Product, Standalone or Integrated, Zoom Support, Google Meet Support, Searchable Transcripts, Action Items, Speaker Identification, Export, Public Pricing / 12-User Monthly Cost, Privacy / Consent / Retention Notes, Best Fit, Limitations
3. Evidence-and-uncertainty ledger
4. Market trends or differentiators
5. Primary recommendation and runner-up
6. Risks and institutional verification items
7. Sources with retrieval dates

google_search_tool.description: Searches the web for task-relevant sources and returns auditable title, URL or DOI, and snippet records.

agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

agent_task.prompt:
Design a high-level architecture for a multi-tenant AI support-ticket triage system. A SaaS company receives about 10,000 tickets per day across 20 enterprise tenants. The system must classify issue type and urgency, redact personal data before every third-party model call, route high-risk or uncertain tickets to human review, preserve tenant-aware audit logs, allow tenant-specific routing rules, meet a 2-second p95 target for automated classification and routing during normal load, and fail safely without dropping tickets.

In addition to the base architecture, analyze these deterministic failure injections:
A. The redaction service returns low confidence for a ticket containing possible PII.
B. The classifier reaches a 1.2-second timeout while queue depth is rising.
C. Two administrators publish conflicting routing-rule versions for the same tenant.

For each injection, state the observed state transition, user/operator visibility, retry or recovery behavior, audit event, and final safe outcome. Include at least three architecture decision records with a selected option, rejected alternative, and rationale. Use only scenario facts and standard architecture reasoning; do not cite vendors, laws, exact costs, or unprovided performance data.
agent_task.required_output_format:
Use the following structure:
1. Assumptions and non-goals
2. Component diagram described in text
3. End-to-end data flow
4. Key services and responsibilities table
5. Tenant isolation and privacy safeguards
6. Reliability and failure-handling plan
7. Latency and throughput reasoning
8. Failure-injection matrix for A, B, and C
9. Architecture decision records
10. Observability, testing, and rollout plan
11. Major risks and mitigations
12. Final recommendation

agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

agent_task.prompt:
Create a 12-week strategic launch plan for a pilot AI course-QA assistant supporting two undergraduate courses and about 300 students. The team has one part-time faculty sponsor, two student developers, one teaching assistant, and a maximum $5,000 pilot budget. The assistant may answer only from instructor-approved course materials and must include safeguards for hallucination, privacy, academic integrity, and escalation to human staff.

Build the baseline plan first. Then apply this required contingency at the end of Week 5: one student developer becomes unavailable during Weeks 6-8, and exactly $750 originally allocated to infrastructure must be reallocated to privacy review and testing. The total budget may not exceed $5,000. Show how scope, ownership, dependencies, testing, support, milestones, and decision gates change; do not merely shift dates.

Define measurable Expand, Revise/Extend, and Stop criteria after Week 12. Do not invent university policies, approvals, completed results, or legal guarantees. Label assumptions.
agent_task.required_output_format:
Use the following structure:
1. Executive summary and assumptions
2. Goals and non-goals
3. Stakeholder and decision-rights map
4. Baseline 12-week roadmap table
5. Baseline resource and budget allocation
6. Week 6-8 contingency overlay and ownership changes
7. Revised budget showing the exact $750 reallocation
8. Risk register and mitigations
9. Success metrics and data-collection plan
10. Governance, content approval, and escalation process
11. Expand / Revise-Extend / Stop decision criteria
12. Final recommendation

agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

agent_task.prompt:
Explain BM25 lexical retrieval, dense embedding retrieval, and a hybrid approach for an internal FAQ system containing 500 documents. Compare matching behavior, synonyms, exact terms and identifiers, exact-phrase handling, data/infrastructure needs, updates, latency, and common failure modes. Be precise that BM25 scoring itself is not the same as positional exact-phrase matching.

Analyze all eight fixed query cases and assign each a BM25-first, Dense-first, or Hybrid route with a brief rationale:
Q1. ERR-1042 reset token expired
Q2. I forgot my password and cannot get back into my account
Q3. What does product code AX-17 mean?
Q4. Please send my billing receipt again
Q5. Disable two-factor authentication for a contractor
Q6. I am not receiving notification emails
Q7. Search for the exact phrase "data export completed"
Q8. How can I move my workspace information to another account?

Then design a lightweight routing/fusion policy for this corpus, provide at least three failure risks with mitigations, and propose a qualitative offline evaluation plan. Do not use external sources, vendors, or invented performance numbers.
agent_task.required_output_format:
Use the following structure:
1. BM25 definition
2. Dense retrieval definition
3. Technical comparison table
4. Eight-query routing table with columns: Query ID, Important Signal, Preferred Route, Rationale, Failure Risk
5. Lightweight hybrid routing/fusion policy
6. Three or more failure risks and mitigations
7. Qualitative offline evaluation plan
8. Final recommendation for 500 FAQ documents

agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

agent_task.prompt:
Conduct a current market comparison of exactly four AI meeting-assistant options for a 12-member university research lab. The set must include at least one platform-integrated option and at least two standalone options. The lab uses Zoom and Google Meet and needs searchable transcripts, action-item extraction, speaker identification, note export, manageable administration, and careful treatment of privacy, participant notice/consent, and data retention.

For every option, verify Zoom and Google Meet support separately. Report current public pricing or plan availability and calculate a 12-user monthly cost only when the public pricing model makes the calculation supportable; otherwise write Not Publicly Computable. Prefer official product, support, pricing, privacy, and retention sources. Include an evidence-and-uncertainty ledger that labels important claims Verified, Partially Verified, or Unknown.

Recommend one primary option and one runner-up. Explain when the runner-up would become preferable. Do not guess unclear pricing, platform support, privacy, consent, compliance, or retention details.
agent_task.required_output_format:
Use the following structure:
1. Brief market context and assumptions
2. Comparison matrix with columns: Product, Standalone or Integrated, Zoom Support, Google Meet Support, Searchable Transcripts, Action Items, Speaker Identification, Export, Public Pricing / 12-User Monthly Cost, Privacy / Consent / Retention Notes, Best Fit, Limitations
3. Evidence-and-uncertainty ledger
4. Market trends or differentiators
5. Primary recommendation and runner-up
6. Risks and institutional verification items
7. Sources with retrieval dates

google_search_tool.description: Searches the web for task-relevant sources and returns auditable title, URL or DOI, and snippet records.

agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

agent_task.prompt:
Design a high-level architecture for a multi-tenant AI support-ticket triage system. A SaaS company receives about 10,000 tickets per day across 20 enterprise tenants. The system must classify issue type and urgency, redact personal data before every third-party model call, route high-risk or uncertain tickets to human review, preserve tenant-aware audit logs, allow tenant-specific routing rules, meet a 2-second p95 target for automated classification and routing during normal load, and fail safely without dropping tickets.

In addition to the base architecture, analyze these deterministic failure injections:
A. The redaction service returns low confidence for a ticket containing possible PII.
B. The classifier reaches a 1.2-second timeout while queue depth is rising.
C. Two administrators publish conflicting routing-rule versions for the same tenant.

For each injection, state the observed state transition, user/operator visibility, retry or recovery behavior, audit event, and final safe outcome. Include at least three architecture decision records with a selected option, rejected alternative, and rationale. Use only scenario facts and standard architecture reasoning; do not cite vendors, laws, exact costs, or unprovided performance data.
agent_task.required_output_format:
Use the following structure:
1. Assumptions and non-goals
2. Component diagram described in text
3. End-to-end data flow
4. Key services and responsibilities table
5. Tenant isolation and privacy safeguards
6. Reliability and failure-handling plan
7. Latency and throughput reasoning
8. Failure-injection matrix for A, B, and C
9. Architecture decision records
10. Observability, testing, and rollout plan
11. Major risks and mitigations
12. Final recommendation

agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

agent_task.prompt:
Create a 12-week strategic launch plan for a pilot AI course-QA assistant supporting two undergraduate courses and about 300 students. The team has one part-time faculty sponsor, two student developers, one teaching assistant, and a maximum $5,000 pilot budget. The assistant may answer only from instructor-approved course materials and must include safeguards for hallucination, privacy, academic integrity, and escalation to human staff.

Build the baseline plan first. Then apply this required contingency at the end of Week 5: one student developer becomes unavailable during Weeks 6-8, and exactly $750 originally allocated to infrastructure must be reallocated to privacy review and testing. The total budget may not exceed $5,000. Show how scope, ownership, dependencies, testing, support, milestones, and decision gates change; do not merely shift dates.

Define measurable Expand, Revise/Extend, and Stop criteria after Week 12. Do not invent university policies, approvals, completed results, or legal guarantees. Label assumptions.
agent_task.required_output_format:
Use the following structure:
1. Executive summary and assumptions
2. Goals and non-goals
3. Stakeholder and decision-rights map
4. Baseline 12-week roadmap table
5. Baseline resource and budget allocation
6. Week 6-8 contingency overlay and ownership changes
7. Revised budget showing the exact $750 reallocation
8. Risk register and mitigations
9. Success metrics and data-collection plan
10. Governance, content approval, and escalation process
11. Expand / Revise-Extend / Stop decision criteria
12. Final recommendation

agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

agent_task.prompt:
Explain BM25 lexical retrieval, dense embedding retrieval, and a hybrid approach for an internal FAQ system containing 500 documents. Compare matching behavior, synonyms, exact terms and identifiers, exact-phrase handling, data/infrastructure needs, updates, latency, and common failure modes. Be precise that BM25 scoring itself is not the same as positional exact-phrase matching.

Analyze all eight fixed query cases and assign each a BM25-first, Dense-first, or Hybrid route with a brief rationale:
Q1. ERR-1042 reset token expired
Q2. I forgot my password and cannot get back into my account
Q3. What does product code AX-17 mean?
Q4. Please send my billing receipt again
Q5. Disable two-factor authentication for a contractor
Q6. I am not receiving notification emails
Q7. Search for the exact phrase "data export completed"
Q8. How can I move my workspace information to another account?

Then design a lightweight routing/fusion policy for this corpus, provide at least three failure risks with mitigations, and propose a qualitative offline evaluation plan. Do not use external sources, vendors, or invented performance numbers.
agent_task.required_output_format:
Use the following structure:
1. BM25 definition
2. Dense retrieval definition
3. Technical comparison table
4. Eight-query routing table with columns: Query ID, Important Signal, Preferred Route, Rationale, Failure Risk
5. Lightweight hybrid routing/fusion policy
6. Three or more failure risks and mitigations
7. Qualitative offline evaluation plan
8. Final recommendation for 500 FAQ documents

agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

agent_task.prompt:
Create a 50-minute introductory lesson plan for undergraduate computer science students on dynamic programming using the minimum-number-of-coins problem with unlimited coin reuse, coin set [1, 3, 4], and target amount 6.

Students already know arrays, loops, and recursion but often confuse greedy algorithms with dynamic programming. Explain when dynamic programming is appropriate, define the subproblem and base cases, derive the recurrence, and build the bottom-up table dp[0] through dp[6] step by step. Explicitly compare the greedy result for amount 6 with the optimal result.

The recurrence, worked table, timeline, activity, misconceptions, quiz, and homework must all use the same minimum-coins variant. End with a compact consistency-audit table that maps each of those artifacts to the state definition and recurrence. Do not use external sources, vendor references, or measured performance claims.
agent_task.required_output_format:
Use the following structure:
1. Lesson title and target audience
2. Learning objectives
3. Prerequisites
4. 50-minute timeline table
5. Concept explanation and recurrence
6. Step-by-step worked dp[0..6] example
7. Board / slide outline
8. In-class activity
9. Common misconceptions and instructor responses
10. Exit quiz with answers
11. Optional homework prompt
12. Consistency-audit table

agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

agent_task.prompt:
Write a concise evidence-based literature review on retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) for reducing hallucination risk in domain-specific question answering.

Use exactly six authentic public scholarly sources published in or after 2020. The six-source set must collectively cover: (1) retrieval/indexing or corpus construction, (2) reranking or context selection, and (3) at least one of citation-grounded generation, answer verification, guardrails, or RAG evaluation. Compare how the families affect factuality, evidence grounding, latency, and implementation complexity.

Include a claim-evidence ledger containing 8-12 substantive claims. For each claim, identify its source and label it Sourced Finding, Synthesis, or Open Question. Identify one real tension or apparent disagreement between the selected sources and resolve it by examining differences in assumptions, tasks, evidence, or evaluation scope. End with exactly three research gaps, each connected to limitations or tensions in at least two of the six sources.

Use public scholarly sources and stable identifiers. Do not fabricate citations, paper details, benchmark results, or disagreements.
agent_task.required_output_format:
Use the following structure:
1. Title
2. One-paragraph executive summary
3. Six-source table with columns: Source, Year, Method / Focus, Key Contribution, Limitation, Stable ID
4. Literature review by the three required method families
5. Tradeoff analysis: factuality, grounding, latency, implementation complexity
6. Claim-evidence ledger (8-12 claims)
7. Apparent disagreement and resolution
8. Exactly three research gaps / future directions
9. Short conclusion
10. References

agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

5. Tenant isolation and privacy safeguards
6. Reliability and failure-handling plan
7. Latency and throughput reasoning
8. Failure-injection matrix for A, B, and C
9. Architecture decision records
10. Observability, testing, and rollout plan
11. Major risks and mitigations
12. Final recommendation

agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

agent_task.prompt:
Create a 12-week strategic launch plan for a pilot AI course-QA assistant supporting two undergraduate courses and about 300 students. The team has one part-time faculty sponsor, two student developers, one teaching assistant, and a maximum $5,000 pilot budget. The assistant may answer only from instructor-approved course materials and must include safeguards for hallucination, privacy, academic integrity, and escalation to human staff.

Build the baseline plan first. Then apply this required contingency at the end of Week 5: one student developer becomes unavailable during Weeks 6-8, and exactly $750 originally allocated to infrastructure must be reallocated to privacy review and testing. The total budget may not exceed $5,000. Show how scope, ownership, dependencies, testing, support, milestones, and decision gates change; do not merely shift dates.

Define measurable Expand, Revise/Extend, and Stop criteria after Week 12. Do not invent university policies, approvals, completed results, or legal guarantees. Label assumptions.
agent_task.required_output_format:
Use the following structure:
1. Executive summary and assumptions
2. Goals and non-goals
3. Stakeholder and decision-rights map
4. Baseline 12-week roadmap table
5. Baseline resource and budget allocation
6. Week 6-8 contingency overlay and ownership changes
7. Revised budget showing the exact $750 reallocation
8. Risk register and mitigations
9. Success metrics and data-collection plan
10. Governance, content approval, and escalation process
11. Expand / Revise-Extend / Stop decision criteria
12. Final recommendation

agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

agent_task.prompt:
Explain BM25 lexical retrieval, dense embedding retrieval, and a hybrid approach for an internal FAQ system containing 500 documents. Compare matching behavior, synonyms, exact terms and identifiers, exact-phrase handling, data/infrastructure needs, updates, latency, and common failure modes. Be precise that BM25 scoring itself is not the same as positional exact-phrase matching.

Analyze all eight fixed query cases and assign each a BM25-first, Dense-first, or Hybrid route with a brief rationale:
Q1. ERR-1042 reset token expired
Q2. I forgot my password and cannot get back into my account
Q3. What does product code AX-17 mean?
Q4. Please send my billing receipt again
Q5. Disable two-factor authentication for a contractor
Q6. I am not receiving notification emails
Q7. Search for the exact phrase "data export completed"
Q8. How can I move my workspace information to another account?

Then design a lightweight routing/fusion policy for this corpus, provide at least three failure risks with mitigations, and propose a qualitative offline evaluation plan. Do not use external sources, vendors, or invented performance numbers.
agent_task.required_output_format:
Use the following structure:
1. BM25 definition
2. Dense retrieval definition
3. Technical comparison table
4. Eight-query routing table with columns: Query ID, Important Signal, Preferred Route, Rationale, Failure Risk
5. Lightweight hybrid routing/fusion policy
6. Three or more failure risks and mitigations
7. Qualitative offline evaluation plan
8. Final recommendation for 500 FAQ documents

agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

agent_task.prompt:
Create a 50-minute introductory lesson plan for undergraduate computer science students on dynamic programming using the minimum-number-of-coins problem with unlimited coin reuse, coin set [1, 3, 4], and target amount 6.

Students already know arrays, loops, and recursion but often confuse greedy algorithms with dynamic programming. Explain when dynamic programming is appropriate, define the subproblem and base cases, derive the recurrence, and build the bottom-up table dp[0] through dp[6] step by step. Explicitly compare the greedy result for amount 6 with the optimal result.

The recurrence, worked table, timeline, activity, misconceptions, quiz, and homework must all use the same minimum-coins variant. End with a compact consistency-audit table that maps each of those artifacts to the state definition and recurrence. Do not use external sources, vendor references, or measured performance claims.
agent_task.required_output_format:
Use the following structure:
1. Lesson title and target audience
2. Learning objectives
3. Prerequisites
4. 50-minute timeline table
5. Concept explanation and recurrence
6. Step-by-step worked dp[0..6] example
7. Board / slide outline
8. In-class activity
9. Common misconceptions and instructor responses
10. Exit quiz with answers
11. Optional homework prompt
12. Consistency-audit table

agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

agent_task.prompt:
Write a concise evidence-based literature review on retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) for reducing hallucination risk in domain-specific question answering.

Use exactly six authentic public scholarly sources published in or after 2020. The six-source set must collectively cover: (1) retrieval/indexing or corpus construction, (2) reranking or context selection, and (3) at least one of citation-grounded generation, answer verification, guardrails, or RAG evaluation. Compare how the families affect factuality, evidence grounding, latency, and implementation complexity.

Include a claim-evidence ledger containing 8-12 substantive claims. For each claim, identify its source and label it Sourced Finding, Synthesis, or Open Question. Identify one real tension or apparent disagreement between the selected sources and resolve it by examining differences in assumptions, tasks, evidence, or evaluation scope. End with exactly three research gaps, each connected to limitations or tensions in at least two of the six sources.

Use public scholarly sources and stable identifiers. Do not fabricate citations, paper details, benchmark results, or disagreements.
agent_task.required_output_format:
Use the following structure:
1. Title
2. One-paragraph executive summary
3. Six-source table with columns: Source, Year, Method / Focus, Key Contribution, Limitation, Stable ID
4. Literature review by the three required method families
5. Tradeoff analysis: factuality, grounding, latency, implementation complexity
6. Claim-evidence ledger (8-12 claims)
7. Apparent disagreement and resolution
8. Exactly three research gaps / future directions
9. Short conclusion
10. References

agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

google_search_tool.description: Searches the web for task-relevant sources and returns auditable title, URL or DOI, and snippet records.

agent_task.prompt:
Conduct a current market comparison of exactly four AI meeting-assistant options for a 12-member university research lab. The set must include at least one platform-integrated option and at least two standalone options. The lab uses Zoom and Google Meet and needs searchable transcripts, action-item extraction, speaker identification, note export, manageable administration, and careful treatment of privacy, participant notice/consent, and data retention.

For every option, verify Zoom and Google Meet support separately. Report current public pricing or plan availability and calculate a 12-user monthly cost only when the public pricing model makes the calculation supportable; otherwise write Not Publicly Computable. Prefer official product, support, pricing, privacy, and retention sources. Include an evidence-and-uncertainty ledger that labels important claims Verified, Partially Verified, or Unknown.

Recommend one primary option and one runner-up. Explain when the runner-up would become preferable. Do not guess unclear pricing, platform support, privacy, consent, compliance, or retention details.
agent_task.required_output_format:
Use the following structure:
1. Brief market context and assumptions
2. Comparison matrix with columns: Product, Standalone or Integrated, Zoom Support, Google Meet Support, Searchable Transcripts, Action Items, Speaker Identification, Export, Public Pricing / 12-User Monthly Cost, Privacy / Consent / Retention Notes, Best Fit, Limitations
3. Evidence-and-uncertainty ledger
4. Market trends or differentiators
5. Primary recommendation and runner-up
6. Risks and institutional verification items
7. Sources with retrieval dates

google_search_tool.description: Searches the web for task-relevant sources and returns auditable title, URL or DOI, and snippet records.

agent_effective_config.description: Legacy OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.

agent_task.prompt:
Design a high-level architecture for a multi-tenant AI support-ticket triage system. A SaaS company receives about 10,000 tickets per day across 20 enterprise tenants. The system must classify issue type and urgency, redact personal data before every third-party model call, route high-risk or uncertain tickets to human review, preserve tenant-aware audit logs, allow tenant-specific routing rules, meet a 2-second p95 target for automated classification and routing during normal load, and fail safely without dropping tickets.

In addition to the base architecture, analyze these deterministic failure injections:
A. The redaction service returns low confidence for a ticket containing possible PII.
B. The classifier reaches a 1.2-second timeout while queue depth is rising.
C. Two administrators publish conflicting routing-rule versions for the same tenant.

For each injection, state the observed state transition, user/operator visibility, retry or recovery behavior, audit event, and final safe outcome. Include at least three architecture decision records with a selected option, rejected alternative, and rationale. Use only scenario facts and standard architecture reasoning; do not cite vendors, laws, exact costs, or unprovided performance data.
agent_task.required_output_format:
Use the following structure:
1. Assumptions and non-goals
2. Component diagram described in text
3. End-to-end data flow
4. Key services and responsibilities table
5. Tenant isolation and privacy safeguards
6. Reliability and failure-handling plan
7. Latency and throughput reasoning
8. Failure-injection matrix for A, B, and C
9. Architecture decision records
10. Observability, testing, and rollout plan
11. Major risks and mitigations
12. Final recommendation
